definition: A system is in C* exactly when accessible evidence establishes that it presents alternatives, those alternatives admit standards-based appraisal, appraisal can alter which response is permitted or warranted, the assessment-relevant facts are finitely specifiable, and the assessment is repeatable in principle.

membership_criteria.statement: the system presents at least two distinguishable response options in some assessment context
at least one option can be assessed as permitted, prohibited, better supported, or worse supported under an explicitly supplied standard
changing the supplied grounds or standard can change which response is permitted or warranted
the facts required for one membership adjudication can be stated in a finite test instance
the same stated instance and standard determine the same membership result in principle

exclusion_rules: mere physical change without assessable alternatives is outside C*
mere prediction without standards-based appraisal is outside C*
mere use of language or symbols is insufficient
membership cannot depend on an RCCD label or RCCD-equivalent vocabulary
unknown_rule: If evidence needed to evaluate any criterion is inaccessible, the case is Unknown rather than in scope or out of scope.
neutrality_obligations: no RCCD component appears in the membership definition
membership is decidable from criterion evidence without inspecting an RCCD mapping
both RCCD-positive and RCCD-unadjudicated systems can satisfy the membership predicate
failure of an RCCD proof cannot retroactively remove a system from C*
class membership and theorem satisfaction are separate predicates
closure_rules: isomorphism of the finite membership instance preserves membership
implementation medium does not determine membership
composition preserves membership only when the composed system itself satisfies all criteria
subsystems are not inherited into C* automatically
nonclaims: C* contains every conceivable form of reasoning
inaccessible systems satisfy or refute RCCD
the criteria are metaphysically necessary for all meanings of reasoning
actual human or machine cognition is fully captured
